strengths: Currently building MCP-based agentic frameworks and AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, providing direct hands-on experience with the agentic tooling and orchestration patterns central to this role.

Production-grade distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic — microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O — establishes the backend depth the role demands.

Fintech-adjacent exposure across RIDE Capital (investment platform, TDD-disciplined delivery) and Optilyz (multi-country pricing optimization) combined with 16+ years of polyglot, stack-agnostic engineering demonstrates the pragmatic judgment an AI-native fintech architecture requires.
concerns: No documented engineering management or team-scaling history; the role requires a track record of growing teams from 10 to 30+ people and developing internal technical leadership layers.
Current agentic AI work appears to be developer tooling and framework infrastructure rather than production multi-agent systems operating under regulated, high-stakes financial constraints.
No evidence of board-level or executive communication, governance of outsourced engineering partners, or P&L-adjacent technical strategy — all explicitly required at this seniority.
response.justification: Aisha brings a rare combination of current, hands-on agentic AI framework work and a long track record operating production distributed systems at significant scale, making her technically credible on the two hardest parts of this mandate — multi-agent architecture and high-throughput backend infrastructure. Her fintech-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital and Optilyz, paired with deep engineering craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, observability), signals the disciplined delivery culture this CTO role must instil; a motivated candidate with this technical foundation could close the executive leadership gap rapidly with the right organizational support.
